title: [FIRRTL] Weird IMCP output w/ name preservation
body:
In the following, I would expect the wire `test` and module output `value` to have the same value.  Instead, `test` is set to a register which is set to a random value, while the module output `value` is set to `0`.
```
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input clock: Clock
    input reset: UInt<1>
    output value: UInt<1>

    inst other of Other

    reg shared : UInt<1>, clock with :
      reset => (other.zero, UInt<1>(1)) @[Broadcast.scala 531:20]

    wire test : UInt<1>
    test <= shared

    value <= test

  module Other:
    output zero: UInt<1>
    zero <= UInt<1>(0)
 ```
 
 ```verilog
module Foo(
  input  clock,
         reset,
  output value);

  reg  shared;
  wire test;

  `ifndef SYNTHESIS
    `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
      reg [31:0] _RANDOM;

    `endif
    `ifdef FIRRTL_BEFORE_INITIAL
      `FIRRTL_BEFORE_INITIAL
    `endif
    initial begin
      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
        _RANDOM = {`RANDOM};
        shared = _RANDOM[0];
      `endif
    end // initial
    `ifdef FIRRTL_AFTER_INITIAL
      `FIRRTL_AFTER_INITIAL
    `endif
  `endif
  assign test = shared;
  Other other ();
  assign value = 1'h0;
endmodule
```
